steps.title: Identify the grammatical need
steps.content: The blank must be the main verb of the sentence, positioned after the subject “Scientists” to introduce the that-clause.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
A finite verb is required as the main verb of an independent clause; “explain” (A) is the finite form that agrees with “scientists” and directly governs the that-clause.

The participles (B, C) and infinitive (D) cannot serve as main verbs of independent clauses.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
